_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize KY + LA statewide-executive batch: jacqueline-coleman (KY Lt Gov, D), russell-coleman (KY AG, R), billy-nungesser (LA Lt Gov, R), liz-murrill (LA AG, R), nancy-landry (LA SoS, R). State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; no party inference.

jacqueline-coleman.notes_set: KY Lt. Governor (D), Beshear running mate, sitting (term to Dec 2027); declared 2027 gubernatorial candidate Apr 20 2026. Documented pro-choice: supports the 'constitutional right to make own reproductive decisions' and post-Dobbs ban exemptions; hosted a panel opposing Planned Parenthood defunding; Beshear-Coleman ticket received heavy PP + NARAL campaign investment (2019 & 2023). Strongest record is opposition to school choice: led the campaign against Amendment 2, 'unequivocally against' diverting public funds. Appeared at a Fairness Campaign LGBTQ rally (not directly quoted -> not scored). No findable position on Christian liberty, elections, justice, federalism, border, or guns (gun-control calls were Beshear's, not hers).

0.note: Pro-choice; supports a woman's 'constitutional right to make her own reproductive decisions' and 'not just access, but autonomy' -- contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocates abortion access and ban exemptions (rape/incest/non-viable) -- the opposite of advocating abolition.

2.note: No evidence on embryonic stem cell / IVF / chimeric.

3.note: No evidence on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: Beshear-Coleman ticket received Planned Parenthood + NARAL campaign investment (2019 & 2023); she publicly opposed PP defunding -- abortion-industry support.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition.

2.note: No evidence on transgender ideology / biological sex.

4.note: Present at a Fairness Campaign LGBTQ rally but not directly quoted -> insufficient to score.

0.note: Led the statewide campaign against Amendment 2; 'public dollars should stay in public schools,' 'unequivocally against' school-choice funding.
No evidence.
No evidence.
No evidence.
No evidence.
No evidence.
No evidence.
No evidence (red-flag/background-check calls were Gov. Beshear's, not hers).

russell-coleman.notes_set: KY Attorney General (R), sitting since Jan 2024 (term to ~Jan 2028); former U.S. Attorney W.D. Ky. Deep cited record: defended KY's fertilization-based 'human being' definition (motion to reconsider, May 2026), SCOTUS mifepristone brief, abortion-pill civil investigation/subpoenas; co-led the multistate suit vs Biden's 2024 Title IX gender rule + joined Skrmetti amicus defending youth gender-care bans; led an 18-state coalition backing LA's Ten Commandments law + opined KY public display is constitutional; child-safety (Character.AI suit, child sex-doll-ban defense, HB 227); Violent Crime Reduction Team + emergency appeal to hold 240+ violent offenders; joined the 26-state Alien Enemies Act deportation brief. NOTE: the pre-2024 Second Amendment / Dobbs amicus briefs on the KY AG site are Cameron-era, NOT Coleman -> guns and anti-ESG left null (no confirmed Coleman-era action). His IVF carve-out (arguing the abortion ban does not reach IVF embryo disposal) is a litigation-scope nuance -> left null rather than over-read as a FALSE.

0.note: Defended KY's statutory 'human being' definition (life from fertilization); filed a May 2026 motion to reconsider calling the ruling against it a 'manifest error of law.'

1.note: Aggressively defends/enforces KY's near-total abortion ban: SCOTUS mifepristone brief + civil investigation and subpoenas over abortion-pill distribution into KY.

2.note: Argued KY's ban does NOT reach IVF embryo disposal -- a litigation-scope position, not a clear policy stance for/against embryo discard; left null.

4.note: No campaign-finance evidence of abortion-industry funding either way.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition.

1.note: No evidence on same-sex marriage law.

2.note: Co-led the multistate suit vs Biden's 2024 Title IX gender-identity rule; joined the Skrmetti amicus + defends KY's minor gender-care ban (Doe v. Thornbury).

4.note: Led the GOP effort against the Title IX rule expanding gender-identity protections in schools.

0.note: Issued a neutral advisory that public resources can't advocate for/against Amendment 2 -- no personal school-choice position.

1.note: Parental-rights opinion on educator communications is not specific to medical/gender consent -> null.

2.note: No clear evidence on CRT/SOGI/sex-ed curricula.

3.note: Defended KY's child sex-doll ban (HB 207) as unprotected speech; applauded HB 227 age-verification/parental controls for under-16; sued Character.AI over child safety.

0.note: No direct statement on publicly professing Christ.

2.note: No clean compelled-speech/pronoun-mandate evidence (counted the Title IX action under marriage).

3.note: Led an 18-state coalition backing LA's Ten Commandments classroom law; opined KY public Ten Commandments display is constitutional.

0.note: No evidence on CBDC/crypto.

4.note: No confirmed Coleman-era anti-ESG action (the cited KY ESG opinion was Cameron-era).

0.note: No position on paper ballots/machines (runs a fraud hotline, but no cell-specific stance).
Stood up a Violent Crime Reduction Team embedded with LMPD and the Jefferson Co. CA -- affirmative pro-police partnership.

1.note: Filed an emergency appeal to keep 240+ violent offenders behind bars; anti-soft-on-crime posture.

2.note: Pursues proportional punishment (240+ offenders appeal) and opioid victim restitution (Purdue multistate settlement).

0.note: No 10th-Amendment nullification act.

1.note: Co-led the suit to block the Biden Title IX federal mandate.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on education (Title IX) and life (mifepristone).

3.note: Pattern of multistate suits affirming state authority against federal action.

4.note: No evidence on federal capture of elections/data/land.

0.note: No position on border barrier/military.

1.note: Joined the 26-state amicus backing use of the Alien Enemies Act to deport Tren de Aragua migrants; 'mandate to secure our border.'

0.note: No confirmed Coleman-era gun action (KY AG-site 2A briefs are 2021 Cameron-era).

billy-nungesser.notes_set: LA Lieutenant Governor (R), sitting since 2016, won a 3rd term outright Oct 2023 (65.5%); office is tourism/culture, so the policy record is thin by design. Most positions come from his own 2023 iVoterGuide questionnaire (self-reported -> HARD): pro-life + anti-PP-funding, man-woman marriage definition, religious-liberty/Judeo-Christian values, balanced-budget amendment, photo voter ID, border wall + anti-sanctuary funding, opposes more gun control. MATERIAL CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (LEFT): publicly opposed/warned against the 2021 transgender-athlete sports ban and other LGBTQ restrictions on tourism grounds ('open our doors very wide,' 'steer clear') -- directly conflicts with his own marriage/SOGI answers; scored FALSE on biblical_marriage q2 and q4. He declined the 2023 governor's race and ran for re-election; next Lt-Gov election 2027.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree' human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection at every stage.

1.note: No evidence on abolition vs restrictions.

4.note: Supports defunding PP (stance), but no campaign-finance evidence for the 'never accepted' cell.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Agree' government should define marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: No explicit position on civil unions/domestic partnerships in law.

2.note: Publicly opposed the 2021 transgender-athlete sports ban -- 'They will have a negative impact... better for us to open our doors very wide.'

4.note: Said LA should 'steer clear' of LGBTQ restrictions to protect tourism -- opposing, not supporting, restraints on LGBTQ promotion.

0.note: Opposes free college, but no K-12 school-choice/homeschool position -> null.
iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree' religious liberty deserves the highest protection and Judeo-Christian values framed our limited government.
No evidence on CBDC/crypto.

2.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree' Congress should pass a balanced-budget amendment.

0.note: No position on paper ballots.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Disagree' people should be able to vote without photo ID -- supports photo voter ID.

2.note: iVoterGuide: 'Agree' deny state/federal funds to non-compliant sanctuary cities.

0.note: No constitutional-carry-specific statement.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'Disagree' more restrictive gun control is needed -- opposes new gun restrictions.

liz-murrill.notes_set: LA Attorney General (R), first woman in the office, sitting since Jan 2024; former LA Solicitor General. Deep, uniformly conservative cited litigation record: Louisiana v. FDA (mifepristone) + first-in-nation extradition of an out-of-state abortion-pill doctor; vigorous defense of LA's minor gender-care ban + Skrmetti/Title IX work; lead defender of LA's Ten Commandments classroom law (HB 71, won 5th Cir. en banc); 23-state anti-ESG coalition; Louisiana v. EAC proof-of-citizenship suit; sought to overturn Roper (juvenile death penalty); sued Biden DHS/ICE/CDC, census-apportionment suit, defended Trump birthright-citizenship EO + challenged New Orleans sanctuary policies; sued the ATF (private-sale + Frame-or-Receiver rules) and was thanked by the NRA for defending LA constitutional carry. No contrary evidence found. Next regular AG election 2027 (no announced bid yet).

0.note: Signed a first-in-nation extradition warrant for an out-of-state doctor who mailed abortion pills into LA -- treating unborn life as protected under LA's near-total ban.

1.note: Filed Louisiana v. FDA to reinstate restrictions on mail-order/telehealth mifepristone; joined the 14-AG effort to preempt abortion 'shield laws' -- aggressive enforcement of LA's near-total ban.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage definition.

1.note: No evidence on same-sex marriage law.

2.note: 'Vigorously defending' LA's ban on gender-transition care for minors; joined Skrmetti + the 25-state brief backing the youth sex-change ban.

4.note: Sued the Biden administration over Title IX transgender-student rules -- 'these rules eviscerate Title IX.'

0.note: No evidence on school choice.

1.note: Act 456 (social-media parental consent) is not medical/gender consent -> null.

3.note: Defended Act 456 (age-verification/parental consent for under-16) and sued Roblox over child exploitation -- age-verification + penalties to protect minors from harmful content.

0.note: No direct profession-of-faith statement.

3.note: Lead defender of LA's Ten Commandments classroom law (HB 71); won 5th Cir. en banc vacatur of the injunction (12-6, Feb 2026); pledged free legal defense to schools.

0.note: No evidence on CBDC/crypto.

4.note: Joined a 23-state coalition questioning the lawfulness of credit-rating agencies' ESG policies.

0.note: No paper-ballot position.

1.note: Filed Louisiana v. EAC to add proof-of-citizenship to the federal voter-registration form; backed the SAVE Act + RNC amicus.

4.note: No evidence on private election funding.

0.note: Prosecutes corruption/crime but no affirmative pro-police-advocacy act to meet the high bar -> null.

2.note: Asked SCOTUS to overturn Roper v. Simmons (restore juvenile death penalty) -- maximal proportional punishment.

0.note: No explicit nullification act.

1.note: Sued Biden DHS/ICE/CDC and won a TRO blocking release of a TB-positive illegal migrant; sued over the Title IX mandate.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on education (Title IX) and life (mifepristone).

3.note: Multistate suit to bar counting illegal aliens in census apportionment; campaign frames her mission as defending LA sovereignty.

4.note: Louisiana v. EAC resists federal capture of state election administration (proof-of-citizenship on the federal form).

0.note: No border-barrier/military position.

1.note: ICE-cooperation enforcement; won a TRO blocking a migrant release and vowed action against those impeding ICE.

2.note: Challenging New Orleans sanctuary policies and moving to dissolve the OPSO ICE-limiting consent decree.

4.note: Defended Trump EO 14160 ending birthright citizenship for children of illegal aliens.

0.note: NRA thanked her for defending LA constitutional carry against Lafayette's attempted end-run.

2.note: Sued the ATF over the private-sale rule and joined the 27-state coalition vs the Frame-or-Receiver rule -- opposing federal gun regulation.

4.note: Litigated against ATF overreach (private-sale + Frame-or-Receiver rules).

nancy-landry.notes_set: LA Secretary of State (R), LA's chief election officer, sitting since Jan 2024; prior LA House (Lafayette, 2008-2019), House Education Chair. Strongest cited record is in her actual portfolio -- election integrity (SAVE citizenship verification + 403 noncitizen removals, mail-ballot limits + anti-harvesting 'one helper' rule) -- and school choice (LA Federation for Children 'Education Choice Champion,' House Ed Chair). MATERIAL CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (LEFT): as a state rep she voted (May 2015) to TABLE/kill the Marriage and Conscience Act (HB 707), a religious-liberty/conscience bill -> scored FALSE on christian_liberty q1; repeatedly characterized as a 'moderate' R. NOTE: a 2019 heartbeat-bill (SB 184) co-sponsorship was search-surfaced but UNVERIFIED on the enrolled bill -> left null (not scored). DISAMBIGUATION: gun/NRA/Zuckerbucks items in search are Gov./AG Jeff Landry, NOT Nancy -> guns left null. Next SoS election ~2027.

0.note: LA Family Forum award recognition is general/SOFT; the 2019 heartbeat-bill sponsorship is unverified -> null.

1.note: Heartbeat-bill (SB 184) co-sponsorship unverified on the enrolled bill -> not scored.

0.note: No direct marriage-definition statement.
School-choice champion: LA Federation for Children 'Education Choice Champion' (2014, 2015); as House Ed Chair pushed school choice -- 'your destiny should not be determined by your ZIP code.'
No evidence.

1.note: Voted (May 2015) to TABLE/kill the Marriage and Conscience Act (HB 707), a conscience-exemption bill protecting marriage-belief actors -- documented vote against a religious-liberty protection.

0.note: No evidence on CBDC/crypto.

4.note: Pro-business (NFIB/LABI) recognition is general -> does not map to WEF/ESG.

0.note: Procuring new voting machines with a paper-trail component (and risk-limiting audits) -- not hand-counted paper / not opposing machines, so q0 unmet -> null.

1.note: Used the SAVE database to verify citizenship and removed 403 voters who failed to prove citizenship; backs HB 691 tying rolls to SAVE.

2.note: Mail ballots limited to 12 verified exemptions -- absentee restricted to specific exceptions.

3.note: Anti-harvesting: 'Only one person is allowed to help you with your ballot to curb ballot harvesting.'

4.note: LA's Zuckerbucks ban predates her tenure and was driven by then-AG Jeff Landry -> null for Nancy.

0.note: No evidence.
No evidence.
No border/military position (noncitizen-voting work is scored under elections).
No evidence for Nancy (gun/NRA hits are Gov./AG Jeff Landry).
